math_recheck: Re-derived against arXiv 1703.04730 LaTeX (approach/efficient/approx/appendix) and the canonical kohpangwei/influence-release code: M-estimation I_up,params=-H^{-1}gradL, I_up,loss, logistic special case (gradL=-sigma(-ytheta^Tx)yx, H=(1/n)sigma sigma xx^T), input-perturbation signs ((1/n) discrete vs -(1/n) continuous), App-B Newton decomposition, SmoothHinge t->0 limit, LiSSA recursion (cur=v+(1-damping)cur-Hcur/scale, /scale), CG quadratic 1/2 t^T H t - v^T t (matches code line 549, fixes the paper's missing-1/2 typo), HVP=grad(v.gradL); all correct. Found the grad_influence_wrt_input return-sign: code returns +s_test^T grad_x grad_theta L = -I_pert,loss (matches canonical influence_op), so docstring/attack-sign was wrong.
fixes: answer.md:129-130 grad_influence_wrt_input docstring corrected: function returns s_test^T grad_x grad_theta L = -I_pert,loss; attack step is x<-Pi(x - a*sign(returned)) (was mislabeled as +I_pert,loss with x+a*sign)
reasoning.md:166-167 same return-sign / attack-direction fix in the code comment
answer.md:56 prose 'influence as s_test*grad/n' corrected: influence_on_test_loss returns the dot product -s_test*grad (=I_up,loss, no /n); removal effect is -(1/n)I_up,loss=(1/n)s_test*grad
caveat: none — all load-bearing formulas verified against the retrieved primary source and canonical implementation; the '0<H<=I' Neumann condition and the 1/2 CG factor were kept consistent with the LiSSA paper and canonical code respectively (the latter intentionally diverges from a typo in the primary text).
